text:
I'm trying to find the last project photo htat was uploaded by a customer in my django project:

```
 p = ProjectPhoto.objects.filter(project__current_shopping_cart__isnull=True).exclude(tag='').order_by('-created_at').first()
```
I want to add a Q expression or outerref(?) that matches `p.photo.created_by.email` with `p.project.customer.legal_contact.email`


To compare fields across related models using Q expressions and OuterRef, you can do it in a few ways. Here are a couple of approaches:

1. Using annotate and SubQuery:
```python
from django.db.models import Q, OuterRef, Subquery

p = ProjectPhoto.objects.annotate(
    customer_email=Subquery(
        Project.objects.filter(
            id=OuterRef('project_id')
        ).values('customer__legal_contact__email')[:1]
    )
).filter(
    Q(project__current_shopping_cart__isnull=True) &
    Q(photo__created_by__email=F('customer_email'))
).exclude(
    tag=''
).order_by('-created_at').first()
```

2. Using a more direct approach with Q objects:
```python
p = ProjectPhoto.objects.filter(
    Q(project__current_shopping_cart__isnull=True) &
    Q(photo__created_by__email=F('project__customer__legal_contact__email'))
).exclude(
    tag=''
).order_by('-created_at').first()
```

If you want to make sure both emails are present and match:
```python
from django.db.models import F

p = ProjectPhoto.objects.filter(
    Q(project__current_shopping_cart__isnull=True) &
    Q(photo__created_by__email__isnull=False) &
    Q(project__customer__legal_contact__email__isnull=False) &
    Q(photo__created_by__email=F('project__customer__legal_contact__email'))
).exclude(
    tag=''
).order_by('-created_at').first()
```

Choose the approach that best fits your needs. The second approach is simpler and should work if your relationships are properly set up. The first approach might be useful if you need more complex conditions or want to handle the comparison differently.

Make sure your model relationships are properly defined for these queries to work:
- ProjectPhoto should have a relationship to Project
- Project should have a relationship to Customer
- Customer should have a relationship to LegalContact
- Photo should have a relationship to User (created_by)
summary: Django Filter Last Matching Photo Email
